pi_items.source: Standard 96-well microplate format defined in ANSI/SLAS standards as 8 rows × 12 columns
ANSI/SLAS 4-2004 specifies 9 mm center-to-center well spacing for 96-well plates
ANSI/SLAS 4-2004 well position standard confirms 14.38 mm distance from left edge to column 1 well center
Standard well position offset of 11.24 mm from top edge, expressed as negative Y in coordinate system where (0,0) is top-left and Y extends downward to -85.48

Standard 96-well plates documented with diameters ranging 6.35-6.94 mm (radius 3.175-3.47 mm). Task specifies 3.25 mm radius (6.5 mm diameter), which is within range but not matching most common 6.4 mm (3.2 mm radius) specification.
AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color 7 = White (RGB 255,255,255)
AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color 4 = Cyan (RGB 0,255,255)
AutoCAD Color Index (ACI) standard: color 2 = Yellow (RGB 255,255,0)

privileged_info_summary: The microplate calibration task specifies dimensions from ANSI/SLAS 1-2004 standard: 127.76 mm × 85.48 mm footprint, 96 wells in 8×12 grid with 9 mm pitch spacing. A1 well center is positioned at (14.38, -11.24) mm from top-left corner with negative Y coordinate system. Standard layer colors use AutoCAD Color Index: OUTLINE=White(7), WELLS=Cyan(4), LABELS=Yellow(2). Well radius specification of 3.25 mm (diameter 6.5 mm) falls within the documented range for standard microplates but differs from the most commonly cited 6.4 mm diameter. All geometric specifications are consistent with the ANSI/SLAS 1-2004 and 4-2004 microplate standards.
